data.description:
## Context
With the bash→Python strangler-fig migration complete (story adult-oxide-slave; E7-final deleted the bash engine at e3507895, rebar is one Python implementation behind three facades), the test suite and CI need a quality pass. The suite grew incrementally across the migration (characterize→port→parity), so it carries change-detector tests, uneven 3-interface coverage, and ad-hoc organization. CI lint is also currently RED on main (pre-existing acli.py F811) and lint/format/test wiring needs to be made reliable.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Recommended ruff lint + format config adopted; lint + format-check + full test suite all gate in CI and run locally (consistency + reliability)
- [ ] Mutation testing run; change-detector (assertion-free / tautological) tests identified and pruned
- [ ] Full coverage of behaviors, integrations, and contracts (internal + external) across ALL three interfaces — MCP, CLI, library
- [ ] Tests organized along logical seams: each test file manageable; each directory 6–12 files (hard cap 24); subdirectories along seams for discoverability; all green locally AND in CI; test isolation maintained
- [ ] Opus SDET-primed review loop run until zero important/critical findings remain (remediating each round)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All five child tasks closed
- [ ] CI is green (lint + format + full suite) on a clean checkout
- [ ] A final SDET review reports no important/critical findings

## Sequencing
CI/lint/format foundation first (so reorg + coverage work is verifiable in CI), then mutation-prune, then coverage gaps, then reorg along seams, then the SDET review loop as the closing gate.
data.title: Post-migration test & CI quality hardening
